questions: Why is the man returning the sweater?
What will the woman most likely do next?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hi. I need to return this sweater. I bought it yesterday, but it's too small for me.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sure. Do you have the receipt?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yes, I have it right here.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Great. We can process the return. Do you want to exchange it for a larger size or get a refund?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'd like to exchange it, please.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: No problem. I will get another in that color for you from our stock.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Thank you.

questions: What is the man doing later today?
What is the woman likely to do next?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Do you have time to do me a favor?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm about to run to the post office, but I can help you after that.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'm hosting this dinner party tonight and I need to pick up some last minute groceries.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: What do you still need?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I need vegetables and ingredients for a dessert. Here's a list.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: No problem. I can swing by the market after I've mailed my package.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You're a lifesaver. Thanks a million.

questions: What are the man and the woman talking about?
What does the man imply when he says, "I'll bring a blanket too"?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Listen to a conversation

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Have you heard about the concert that the university orchestra is putting on in the park? I know you're a big fan of classical music.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I am. And I'm already going with a group of friends. You should come with us.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Thanks, I'd love to. So how much are tickets?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You only have to pay if you sit in the seats up close near the stage. We're all going to bring blankets and just sit on the grass a little farther back.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good idea. I'll bring a blanket too.

questions: What is the man's problem?
At the end of the conversation, what is the man's attitude toward the woman's idea?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I was just typing my paper and my computer suddenly shut down.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You've had a lot of problems with that laptop, haven't you?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried holding down the power button for a few seconds?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I did, but nothing happened. I'd rather not take it to a repair shop, but I don't know what else to do.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How about you get in touch with my ex roommate, Monica? She's a real tech whiz.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that's an idea. Could you give me her number? I'm not sure I have it anymore.

questions: What can be inferred about the bakery?
Based on the conversation, what will happen during the interview?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: This is Jimmy Clerken from Rise Bakery calling. I received a message that you'd like to do a story about my shop for your university newspaper.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yes. You've been a staple in our city for generations now. The grandparents of our current students were customers there when they were young.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's a family owned business and we're proud of it. Would you be able to come next Monday at 3 o'clock?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Definitely. Is it all right if my photographer comes along?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Of course. You can get different shots, even back in our kitchen where we do all the baking.

A Have you heard about the concert that the university orchestra is putting on in the park? I know you're a big fan of classical music. B: I am and I'm already going with a group of friends. You should come with us. A: Thanks, I'd love to, so how much your tickets? B: You only have to pay if you sit in the seats up close near the stage. We're all going to bring blankets and just sit on the grass a little farther back. A: Good idea, I'll bring a blanket too.

A: Hi, I received a parking ticket at the Smith Street parking garage yesterday. It said I was parked in an area reserved for faculty. B: Were you parked on the first level? A: Yes, but I've been parking there for months now. I thought it was a student area. B: It was, but we changed it last week. There are signs posted outside the garage. A: Oh honestly, I probably wasn't paying attention anyway. Can I go ahead and pay it now?

A: I need to buy a birthday present for my friend who loves cooking. Do you have any ideas? B: How about a high quality set of kitchen knives? A: That's a good idea, but I think she has a good set of knives already. She cooks a lot so she has all the basic equipment. B: Maybe you should stop by the Kitchenware store downtown and ask their advice. I bet they have a lot of fun stuff for people who already have a well stocked kitchen.

A: Did you hear about the mural project our universities doing? I really think you should enter. B: Wait, what mural project? C: They want to create a mural on the side of the student center. Students will submit paintings and the judges will select the top five, and then those students will lead the official mural committee. A: Are you doing it? The paintings you've done in our class are amazing. B: I already have the newspaper club and student council. A:Well, it sounds interesting. I'll look into it.

items.setting: Listen to a conversation? A: Wasn't there supposed to be a yoga class in the conference room at noon today? I stopped in right at noon and no one was there.

conversation.text: I was really looking forward to it. B: Didn't you get the e-mail? The instructor had a personal conflict and had to cancel. A: When did she send it? I don't know, maybe 11:15? B: That makes sense. I've been away from my desk since mid morning and haven't checked my e-mail. A: Then you probably don't know that she is rescheduled for tomorrow.

I just interviewed the director of campus housing for the university newspaper, and she confirmed the rumors. The cost to live in the dormitories will be about 10% higher next year. Oh, wow. That's a lot. I'm wondering if it'd make sense to start looking for an apartment or something that's not on campus. That's what I'm planning to do. If I come across any good ones I'm not taking, I'll pass them along to you. Awesome. Thanks.

Have you heard about the concert that the university orchestra is putting on in the park? I know you're a big fan of classical music. I am. And I'm already going with a group of friends. You should come with us. Thanks, I'd love to. So how much are tickets? You only have to pay if you sit in the seats up close near the stage. We're all going to bring blankets and just sit on the grass a little farther back. Good idea. I'll bring a blanket too.

Hi, this is Sarah calling from the campus tutoring center. I just wanted to confirm your biology session with Raul tomorrow at noon. I'm glad you called. My manager at work needs me to go in tomorrow. I get off at three. Hmm. Okay, let me check the schedule. Raoul's not available at that time, but we could put you in with Kira at 3:30. That'd be perfect. I've worked with her before and she's great. Thank you.

Hi, Ben. Did you get your laptop issue all sorted out? Yeah, I called the IT desk, but couldn't get an appointment till next weekend, so I took it to the place down the street. The technician replaced the faulty ram and it's working great now. Awesome. Did it take a long time? I had it back the same day. The price wasn't bad and the people were super friendly. That's good to know. Yeah. I'll text you the name of the place in case you ever need it.

Are you planning to attend the theater performance this Friday? I am, but I've had so much to do about moving to my new apartment this week that I haven't even had time to look into buying my ticket yet. You should hurry. It's selling out fast. I'll buy my ticket today. The play got some rave reviews, so I'd hate to miss out on it.

Hi, Emily. Have you had any luck with your search for a roommate for your new apartment? Not yet. I've posted ads online, but I haven't received many responses. Have you tried asking around at the university? Your classmates might know someone. That's a good idea. I'll ask around tomorrow. I hope you find someone soon. It's so expensive when you're not sharing the rent. Definitely.

Have you booked your flight for the conference yet? Not yet. Experience tells me that if I wait long enough, I can get a great last minute deal. What happens if there are no tickets left though? You might miss the conference. That's never been a problem before. But I suppose there's always a first time. And I'm meeting professors there to discuss my research. I think you're right. I'd better go get a ticket now.

I'm looking for a good restaurant to celebrate finishing my thesis. Do you have any recommendations? How about that new French restaurant downtown? I went last week and I had a chicken dish that was delicious. That sounds perfect. My advisor loves French food. I'll check if they have any availability for Friday night. You might want to call them soon. I think they fill up fast.

My printer isn't working and I have to print this report now. What's the rush? I have a class at 3 o'clock. Have you checked if it's out of paper or ink? Yes, I did. The paper tray is full and I replaced the ink cartridge yesterday. Maybe it's a software issue. You could try reinstalling the printer driver. That often resolves these kinds of problems. Or you can call the help desk. That will take forever, and it's almost 3 o'clock. I guess I'll just use the printer in the computer lab.

Did you print a copy of your resume for the interview tomorrow? I didn't need to. I submitted it online. That was what they requested. Okay. I had to bring several copies of mine when I applied for that internship, and I met with several people for the interview. What was the position again? In the accounting department. Maybe it's different. In graphic design, I'm only meeting with one person. Oh, that sounds a lot less stressful.

A: Listen to a conversation. B: Hey, Linda. I've been looking all over for you. A: I just got back from lunch. What's up? B: I've been thinking about getting a new couch for my dorm room. Do you have any recommendations? A: Have you checked out Comfort Furniture Store? I shop there all the time. They have a great selection and their prices are reasonable. B: Do you know if they offer delivery services? A: Yes, they do, and they're having a sale starting tomorrow. I'll send you a link to their catalog.

B: Listen to a conversation. A: Are Alyssa and David back from studying abroad in Hong Kong? B: Yes, they returned last week. Alyssa's still adjusting to the time difference. A: I can relate. I had bad jet lag last summer after traveling across the globe. I kept taking long naps, which affected my sleep at night. B: Yeah, instead of napping, getting outside for a walk and sunlight helps reset your internal clock. I told Alyssa the same. A: Once she's over it, let's all have lunch. B: Sounds good. She just needs to sort out credit for an extra course she took over there, then she'll be free.

questions: Why does the woman ask about maintenance on her car?
What will the woman most likely do next?

conversation.text: A: I'm planning a trip to visit my aunt during spring break. What maintenance should I do on my car? B: Make sure to check the tire pressure and oil level. It's also a good idea to have your car inspected by a mechanic before you go. A: That's helpful, Can you recommend a mechanic? There's a garage on Court Street across from the grocery store. I'll give you a link to book an appointment. B: Thanks. I'll take care of that right away.

questions: Why was the man looking for the woman?
What does the woman suggest she will do in the future?

conversation.text: A: I just heard that the art store in the mall is going out of business. B: Oh, no. I get painting supplies for my senior art project there. A: I know. That's why I came looking for you. What will you do now? B: Well, I have a couple of options. I could get them online or I can go to the store in Porterville, 10 miles away. A: Seems to me like you'd want to buy that kind of stuff in person. B: I would. It looks like I'll be making regular trips to Porterville.

questions: What does the woman like about the apartment?
What does the woman say she will do tomorrow?

conversation.text: A: Hey Josie, how's the apartment hunt going? B: I'm so glad you asked. I think I found the one. It's close to campus, which is nice. A: That's awesome. You'll be near the university? That'll be convenient. Did you sign the lease yet? B: Not yet. I'm meeting with the landlord tomorrow. I'll tour the grounds and see the pool too. A: Good luck. I'll keep my fingers crossed.

questions: Why is the man taking a train to the conference?
What does the man suggest that the woman do?

conversation.text: A: Did you say you're taking the train to the conference instead of driving? B: That's right. I'm sure I'll still be working on my conference paper and it will be nice to have some quiet time on the train to complete it. A: That's smart. I was going to drive. B: That's not a bad idea either. That way you'll have your car for getting around while you're there. A: Yes, but driving alone is boring. Maybe I'll take the train too. B: Well be sure to bring a book if you do. 2 hours is a long time on the train.

questions: What does the man imply about a restaurant?
What does the man imply when he mentions a bed?

conversation.text: A: Have you checked out the new restaurant downtown? The sushi is made by an award-winning chef and is absolutely top notch. B: I'd love to, but I'm saving money for a vacation. A: Do you have specific plans? B: Not yet, but I'd love to spend a month somewhere in nature. A: Have you considered a camping trip? You could just pack a tent and a sleeping bag into your car and travel all around the mountains while sleeping at campsites. No need for hotels or plane tickets. B: Well, I do love me a comfortable bed at the end of an eventful day.

A: Hi, Maya. Since today's your first day with us at the Health Center, I'll be training you. Have you ever worked as a receptionist before? B: Yeah, I had a similar job at the campus Health Center at my old university, but only on the weekends. A: Great. OK, so we have this checklist here of items we have to go through. It's rather comprehensive and we won't get to everything today. It's just been updated so it should be pretty easy to use. B: I'm eager to get started.

A: I saw the new painting exhibition in the Art Museum. Have you? B: Moving to my new apartment has taken up all of my free time recently. There's so much packing and unpacking to do. How's the exhibit? A: There's a good variety of paintings and their arrangement is really creative. I just wish it featured some stuff from artists in our area. We have so many talented painters here whose work is worth seeing, but All in all it's quite awesome. A: I'll go check it out this weekend before it closes. I should be all moved in by then.

A: Have you tried using that new app our physics professor mentioned? I was going to download it, but then I figured I wouldn't have time to figure out how to use it. B: I can totally show you. It's not that bad, and it's been helpful for our last couple of homework assignments. A: Great, I was planning to go to the tutoring canter for extra help. B: You probably won't need to now. Here, I'll show you where to get it. And I have the code the professor gave us for the free trial. A: I actually have that.

A: Did you end up seeing that movie yesterday with your friend? B: Yes. It was a good way to spend some time with her. It was an afternoon showing and the place was practically empty. A: How was the movie? B: It was OK. There were lots of unexpected twists, but it was too long. A: Should I see it? B: I'm not sure I'd pay money to see it in the theater. Just wait a few months and it will be out on streaming. A: That's usually how I watch movies these days anyway.

A: So did you manage to get those tickets for the theatre? B: Yes, I got some great seats near the stage for Friday evening. A: That's fantastic. What play did you say it is? B: It's a modern adaptation of Shakespeare's King Lear. A: Oh, that sounds interesting. B: I think so too. I read King Lear in high school, but I've never seen it performed and I find the idea of a modern adaptation really intriguing. I sure hope my sister does too.

A: I'm so excited about our road trip this weekend. I'm all packed and got the food and snacks for the trip. B: I thought David was supposed to prepare snacks. A: No, it was me. David signed up to bring water for everyone. B: I see. A: Are you all packed? B: Yes, I just want to get my car checked before we leave. I'm taking it to the mechanic tomorrow morning. A: Good idea by the way. I just checked and there might be light showers in the afternoon, but overall good weather on our route.

A: Customer support. How may I help you? B: Well my computer keeps overheating and shutting down. A: Have you checked if the fans are working properly? Dust can accumulate and block air flow. B: I haven't looked at that. A: You can get a can of compressed air and spray it into the vents and the fans to blow out the dust. B: OK I'll try that. A: Hopefully that'll take care of the problem. If not, you'll probably need to ship it to us so we can take a look.

A: Welcome to the student housing office. What can I do for you? B: Hi, I'm looking for a 2 bedroom apartment and I wondered if you have some available? A: Yes we do. How much is the rent? B: Standard is $1800 a month but we also have some with a nice view of the lake. Those are 2100 hundred. A: 21 hundred is a bit much. Do you mind if I have a look at one of the standard 2 bedrooms? B: Of course. Let me grab the key from the drawer.

A: I'm so grateful to you for looking after my dog while I'm in the mountains. B: I'm happy I can help. How's the planning for your trip coming? A: I've already booked a flight and a cabin. All I need now is to arrange a rental car. B: Terrific. How long are you staying? A: I'll be in the cabin for one week and then I'll spend another week hiking a trail in the National Park. B: Wow, my vacations are so boring. I really envy you.

A: I bought a traditional recipe book and started learning how to bake. B: That sounds great. How has it been going so far? A: Well, with mixed success, My chocolate cake turned out fabulous, But my cookies, well, let's say I'm not going to have anyone try them. I managed to eat a couple, but only because I have strong teeth. B: You'll do better next time. A: I hope so. In fact, I'm going to try again tonight. I'm headed to the store right now to get some extra flour and sugar.

A: Did you go to the rock concert in the park yesterday afternoon? B: I went to a gardening workshop at the University Center instead. It's sad that I missed out on the concert, but I'm starting my own garden and I need to know all the insurance and outs of growing herbs and vegetables. A: Nice. And to be honest, you didn't miss that much, B: no? A: Well, I love some of the songs they played, but the acoustics in the park aren't very good, so the sound quality was rather mediocre and didn't do justice to the songs.

A: How was the photography workshop last week? B: We learned techniques for taking portraits. Next week we'll do action photography. A: That sounds so interesting. I've always wanted to take pictures of tennis matches. B: The instructor who will lead the class next week has photographed state championships in six different sports. A: I'd love to attend. I'll buy a new camera for the occasion. Are all ability levels welcome? B: Yes, I'll e-mail you the link to sign up. Just be aware that spots are going fast.

A: Are you going to the outdoor concert this Saturday? B: Is it still happening with all the rain in the forecast? A: They said it's on, rain or shine. Plus we might get a dry window in the evening. B: Hmm. I'll think about it. I have a lot of studying to do for my law school exam, but I'd love to see the performance of the headline band, the Rumbling Waves. I grew up in the same neighborhood as their lead singer and even played basketball with him a few times.

A: Is that the subway schedule you're looking at? Is Line 1 running on time? B: It says there might be a delay due to track maintenance. A: Oh no, I hope it's not too long. I have to be at campus by 10 to present and turn in the biology project report. B: I recommend taking an earlier train just to be safe. The delays can be up to an hour. A: Good idea, I'll head that way now.

A: Should we take the train or fly to the conference? B: The train is slower, but the train station is a quick walk from the conference hotel. The airport is at least half an hour away. A: Train it is then. I hope the hotel has nice views of the city. B: That I can't answer. Just be ready to leave at 7 AM. A: 7:00. That's awfully early. Is that the only option? B: I thought we should take an early train so we have time to sightsee when we get there.

A: Hi Lisa, Do you have any plans for the weekend? B: Well, I did want to go to a baseball game in Center City on Saturday, but the weather's not looking too good. A: Yes, storms are forecast all weekend, I think. B: Right, So I decided to go to that new art gallery in town this week. Better to do something indoors. A: Good thinking. I went last week and there were a few sculptures by a local artist that really caught my eye. You need to check them out. B: Oh, nice tip. I can't wait to see them.

A: Are you excited about the music festival this Saturday? Neon Chapel is performing and they're one of my favorite bands. B: Mine too, but my parents are going to be visiting for the weekend and it seems they're not interested. A: Oh I see. B: Yeah, it was a last minute thing. A few of my paintings are on display at the campus Art Museum right now for the student exhibit and they want to see them. A: Oh well, that'll be fun. And you should take your parents to the Italian place on Front St. it's really good.

questions: Why does the woman need to create a budget?
What does the man suggest the woman do with her list of expenses?

conversation.text: B: How's your business class going? Have you been given any assignments yet? A: First one's due on my. Monday. I'm supposed to create a budget for an imaginary company. I've come up with a list of expenses, but I don't know what to do next. B: Now that you've listed your expenses, you should prioritize them. A: That makes sense. There probably won't be enough revenue at first to cover all of my costs. B: That's exactly right. And don't forget to include a contingency fund. You can bet that unexpected costs will arise sooner or later. A: This is a great start. Thanks.

questions: What about the woman surprises the man?
What does the man say he will do tomorrow?

conversation.text: A: I was just over at the student center, and they were showing this awesome documentary about the wildlife in our region. B: I know. I was part of the film crew. You were? We were all sworn to secrecy until the project was completed. I'm glad you liked it. A: Like it? I loved it. It was one of the most amazing programs I've ever seen. The photography was stunning. I'll definitely be there tomorrow night when they show it again.

questions: Why is the man seeking advice?
What does the man imply when he says, "I'll keep a full pitcher of water at my desk at all

conversation.text: A: I've been having trouble concentrating when I study. Do you have any advice? B: Have you tried taking short breaks throughout the day? That can help refresh your mind. A: I usually just keep working until I'm finished with the assignment. Maybe I should try that. B: Also make sure you're staying hydrated and getting enough sleep. Both can greatly affect your concentration. A: I'll keep a full pitcher of water at my desk at all times. times"?

items.setting: Listen to a conversation A: Have you finished that book you were reading on photography techniques.
questions: Why does the man mention practical tips?
What does the man imply he will do?

conversation.text: B; Almost. It's really insightful. A: I've been thinking about picking up photography as a hobby. Do you recommend the book for beginners? B: Definitely. It covers the basics very well and has plenty of practical tips. A: You think they have it at the campus bookstore? Maybe I'll pick up a copy. B: Yeah, that's where I got it. But next week's your birthday, and I haven't gotten you a gift yet, so maybe just wait until then.

questions: What kind of painting did the woman complete?
What does the woman plan to do with her painting?

conversation.text: A: Hi, Lisa. Did you finish your painting? B: Yes, I completed it yesterday. It's a watercolor of a mountain lake at sunrise. Lots of soft colors and reflections. I think it came out well. A: That sounds beautiful. Are you planning to display it anywhere? B: I'm thinking of entering it in the university art show next month. I've never submitted anything before so I'm a little nervous. A: I think you should go for it, it sounds like a piece people would really enjoy. B: Thanks, I hope so. I just need to get it framed first.

A: I've been trying to get in shape, but I'm not having much success know a lot about Fitness. What am I doing wrong? B: The most important thing is staying motivated. A: Yes, that's been a problem for me. B: You should set small. Achievable goals. Seeing your progress can be very motivating. A: I see what you're saying. B: You might also want to think about joining a fitness class, working out with others. Can keep you motivated and accountable. A: That's a great idea. I'll go online now and see what's available.

A: I've been put in charge of food for the University, football teams holiday party, next week. And I don't know where to start. B: How many people will be there? A: Around 40. B: That's a big group. The first thing you need to do is find out people's likes and dislikes and see if anyone has any dietary restrictions. A: That makes sense. How do I go about it? B: Just write up a little questionnaire and send it to everyone. One then based on the responses, right? Up a menu and go shopping. A: Nice. I'll work on that now.

A: My computer keeps freezing whenever I try to open a large file. B: Have you tried restarting it? A: Yes, but it didn't help, I'm worried, it might be a hardware issue. B: Maybe it's time for a memory upgrade that could solve the problem. A: You might be right. I'll take it to a technician to check. B: Good idea. They'll be able to help you.

A: I've been thinking about taking up photography. B; Really. Why photography? A: A friend of mine is really into it and it sounds interesting. B: Just be sure not to spend too much money until you know you want to stick with it. Trust me when I say that photography is an expensive hobby. A: Do you have any tips for a beginner? B: Start by learning the basics of your camera and practicing different techniques. A: That makes sense. Any good online resources? B: There are plenty of free tutorials. You can also join photography forums for feedback and advice from others.

A; Hello, I ordered some school supplies on your website but my classes start Monday and I'm nervous the items won't arrive on time. B: Well, we do offer an expedited option. If you placed your order in the last 24 hours I can still update it. A: Oh that would be great. How much extra is it ? B: $20.00 but you can also sign up for an annual membership $5 a month for unlimited expedited shipping. A: All right. I guess I'll do that then.

A: Hi Lisa, have you managed to set up the new software on your computer? B: Not yet. I tried installing it but I keep getting an error message. A: Did you check if your system meets the software requirements? B: Yes, my computer is definitely compatible so I think it might be a network issue. A: You should contact IT support, they can help you sort it out. B: Good idea. I'll give them a call this afternoon. A: Let me know how it goes.

A: I'm so excited for the outdoor concert this weekend. B: Have you seen the weather forecast? We get soaked. A: Well that's disappointing. I was really looking forward to going. B: Same here. Any thoughts on what to do instead? I A: heard there's a new exhibit opening on Saturday at the art gallery. Tickets go on sale tomorrow. B: Count me in. We can meet up for breakfast that morning too.

A: What do you know about this rock festival that's happening? B: Not much. Do you know what time it starts? A: It kicks off at 3:00 PM. B: That's perfect. Oh, I remember hearing that. There will be food trucks. A: Oh, right. And a dance floor. B: No way. You can't keep us away. Let's get there early so we can get a good spot.

A: Have you heard they're finally updating the kitchen in the student lounge of this dormitory? B: It's about time. What are they doing? A: Well, for one, all of the appliances are being replaced. B: Oh, that's great. The microwave hasn't been working properly for a while. A: I heard they're replacing some of the cabinets too. B: I hope it doesn't take too long. I cook there a lot. A: Me too. I think we'll have access to the place in the dorm next door though.

A: Lisa, you've been taking guitar lessons, right? How's that going? B: It's good. I'm having fun and there are four or five songs I can play pretty well now. A: I've been thinking about it myself. I just finished a big work project so I've got some spare time. B: If you'll wait here a minute I'll give you my teachers information. She's great. A: Do you do it online or in person? B: I took a few lessons in person to get started. It's helpful to have someone show you some basic things, but now we do it online and it works well.

A: I saw your photo of a mother duck with her chicks in the student magazine. That was amazing. B: Oh, thanks. I was lucky to get that close to them. A: Is photography a hobby for you? B: Sort of. It's something I enjoy in my free time, but I don't have much of that. A: You should join the college photography club. We have a street photography workshop coming up that should be really interesting. B: I prefer wildlife, but that does sound like a lot of fun. Can you send me a link to the club's website?
